content: We Work Remotely: Remote jobs in design, programming, marketing and more https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs.rss We Work Remotely: Remote jobs in design, programming, marketing and more en-US 60 Tablet Command, Inc.: Marketing Coordinator Anywhere in the World Sales and Marketing Full-Time <img src="https://we-work-remotely.imgix.net/logos/0171/5343/logo.gif?ixlib=rails-4.0.0&w=50&h=50&dpr=2&fit=fill&auto=compress" /> <p> <strong>Headquarters:</strong> United States <br /><strong>URL:</strong> <a href="https://tabletcommand.com">https://tabletcommand.com</a> </p> <p>Tablet Command builds software that matters. We are the emerging leader in incident response and management solutions for public safety agencies across the US and Canada. Our platform is used by firefighters and first responders in the field, where clarity, speed, and reliability are critical.</p> <p></p> <p>We are looking for a Marketing Coordinator - Content Marketing Specialist with a strong focus on content to support our growing marketing efforts. This role is responsible for creating and executing content that helps communicate our value clearly, supports our sales team, and drives engagement across channels.</p> <p></p> <p>This is a hands-on role. You will work closely with leadership, sales, and subject matter experts to turn real-world insights into content that resonates with our audience.</p> <p></p> <p>Tablet Command is a remote team based in the San Francisco Bay Area. Preferred time zones are US (Hawaii to East Coast).</p> <p></p> <p><strong>Requirements</strong></p> <p>The ideal candidate has around five years of experience in marketing, with a strong focus on content creation in a B2B, SaaS environment, and emergency management service. You are comfortable owning projects end-to-end, working with technical or operational subject matter, and producing clear, practical, engaging content.</p> <p></p> <p>We value clarity, consistency, and relevance in our marketing. The right person for this role knows how to take ideas and make them easy to understand without losing their substance.</p> <p></p> <p>As part of a collaborative and agile team, you will:</p> <p></p> <ul> <li>Own the creation of marketing content including social media, blog posts, emails, one-pagers, website pages, and sales materials</li> <li>Translate product capabilities and field use cases into clear, grounded messaging</li> <li>Manage and maintain the content calendar to ensure consistent output</li> <li>Own SEO at the content level, including keyword research, on-page optimization, and ongoing content improvements</li> <li>Use tools like SEMrush, Google Analytics, GSC, or similar to inform content strategy, identify gaps, and prioritize high-impact opportunities</li> <li>Work directly with internal stakeholders to source ideas and develop content</li> <li>Experience marketing to a defined account universe, with the ability to tailor content strategy, channel selection, and messaging to specific agency types, sizes, or buying stages rather than broad horizontal audiences</li> <li>Build and deploy content within tools like HubSpot or similar platforms</li> <li>Create simple, effective visual assets using tools like Canva</li> <li>Support events and trade show content such as pre and post event follow up.</li> <li>Support campaign execution with relevant, timely content, driving alignment and securing necessary approvals from stakeholders</li> <li>Maintain consistency in voice and messaging across all assets<br><br> </li> </ul> <p>In your application, please include an example of content you've created that you're proud of. This can be a blog, social, email campaign, case study, infographic, or any piece of work that reflects your approach.</p> <p></p> <p><strong>Nice-to-haves include:</strong></p> <p></p> <ul> <li>Experience in public safety, government, or technical SaaS environment</li> <li>Familiarity with HubSpot, CMS platforms, and email creation</li> <li>Experience supporting sales teams with content used in real conversations</li> <li>Ability to work independently and collaborate in a remote team</li> <li>Strong sense of ownership and attention to detail</li> </ul> <p></p> <p><strong>The Application</strong></p> <p></p> <p>If you think you are a good fit for this position, please submit your resume along with an example of content you've created.</p> <p></p> <p>Your resume will be reviewed and, if selected, you will be scheduled for an initial conversation with our team. We'll use this time to learn more about your experience and give you a better sense of how we work.</p> <p></p> <p>If we decide to move forward, you'll meet with additional team members and discuss your approach to content, collaboration, and execution. Final candidates may be asked to complete a short exercise to walk through how they would approach a real-world content scenario.</p> <p></p> <p>The final stage for successful candidates will be a case study/presentation challenge, where you'll walk us through how you'd approach a real-world customer support scenario. This will be followed by a conversation with a team member and will take no longer than 1-2 hours in total. </p> <p></p> <p>f selected, a conditional job offer will be presented that is contingent upon reference and background check and E-Verify.</p> <p><strong>Benefits</strong></p> <p>We're committed to building a company where people can do meaningful work without unnecessary friction. We treat people well by supporting remote work, reasonable work schedules, family time flexibility, and a collaborative environment. We build exceptional support experiences by fostering clear communication, empowering the team to lead decisions, hiring smart people, and letting them get things done. We keep our edge with a commitment to inclusion and professional development.</p> <p></p> <p>The most important part of our company is the human element. No matter what we do, it's guided first and foremost by an obsession with being fair, humane, kind, and respectful - values that extend not just to our team, but to every customer we serve.</p> <p></p> <p>At Tablet Command you will:</p> <ul> <li>Work on software that directly impacts public safety</li> <li>Help communicate ideas that support better outcomes in the field</li> <li>Be part of a small, focused team that values clarity and execution</li> <li>Have the autonomy to own your work and see it through<br> </li> </ul> <p></p> <p>The anticipated salary range for this position is $80,000-$95,000 annually. Starting pay will be based on qualifications, experience, and geographic market considerations, with final compensation determined by market conditions and internal equity.</p> <p></p> <p><strong>At Tablet Command we provide:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Comprehensive medical, dental, and vision insurance with a generous employer contribution (HMO, PPO, and HDHP options)</li> <li>Life insurance at 3x annual salary</li> <li>Short- and long-term disability coverage</li> <li>Pai
